title: Sarcomphalus obtusifolius

intro: Sarcomphalus obtusifolius, also known as Brazilian guanabira or Brazilian guanabira vine, is a woody vine native to the tropical forests of South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to aid in the treatment of fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Sarcomphalus obtusifolius include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, Sarcomphalus obtusifolius is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, or used in combination with other herbs to create remedies for various health conditions.
